design_philosophy.core_loop: Climb vertically through scrolling stages, defeat enemies using martial arts attacks, reach the boss at the top to rescue the hostage
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigating cramped vertical spaces while managing limited attack range against multiple enemies and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Martial artist ascending through a tower/building to rescue a hostage, embodying the Jackie Chan action film experience (Spartan X/Game of Death inspiration)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early arcade-to-console port that prioritizes faithful arcade gameplay translation over console-specific design; rigid vertical progression with no shortcuts; demands pattern learning and precise timing rather than player agency in approach

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with game over condition; player must restart from beginning
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints; each stage must be completed in one attempt or restart entire game
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; fixed enemy spawns and patterns with no difficulty selection or adaptive scaling

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage completion; each completed stage unlocks next stage automatically
replayability_hooks: Pattern memorization rewards mastery across multiple playthroughs
Arcade game design loop encourages speedrun optimization

world_building_techniques: Stage-by-stage ascent through a building implies vertical narrative progression
Enemy types change per stage (thugs, ninjas, etc.) suggesting escalating threat
Final boss encounter implies rescue objective payoff

boss_design.philosophy: Single powerful adversary at stage conclusion that must be defeated to advance; represents the culmination of stage-specific challenge patterns
notable_bosses: Unnamed boss character at each stage summit (specific boss names not documented in sources)

faction_indicators: Enemy hierarchy: basic thugs → specialized fighters (ninjas implied) → final boss
Vertical tower structure suggests power concentration at top
danger_signals: Confined vertical spaces restrict escape routes
environmental_storytelling: Building/tower ascent communicates progress toward goal
Enemy placement and type variation implies different guardians per floor
Top-of-stage boss arena represents final obstacle before objective

sources_used: tcrf - Kung Fu (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
